intro: Zygophyllum minutistipulum, also known as the minute-leaved zygophyll, is a small, succulent shrub native to the dry regions of the Middle East and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Zygophyllum minutistipulum include flavonoids, saponins, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and topical ointments, as well as being incorporated into various traditional remedies, such as salves and liniments.
